# 720 Content Standards

Use this skill for any work involving 720 learning content, content metadata, adaptive content routing, iframe/lomda generation, assessment components, learner feedback, or xAPI reporting.

## Procedure
1. Identify the content level: unit, component, or item.
2. Load the relevant reference:
   - Metadata fields: [metadata schema](./references/metadata-schema.md)
   - xAPI and monitoring: [xAPI reporting](./references/xapi-reporting.md)
   - Closed lists: [closed lists](./references/closed-lists.md)
   - Pedagogy and UX: [pedagogy](./references/pedagogy.md)
3. Check that the content realizes one learning objective, or is explicitly a sub-topic summary unit.
4. Confirm the route shape: closed, modular, or hybrid.
5. Verify learner-facing feedback is formative, non-numeric, specific, and forward-looking.
6. Verify the content can be identified, routed, measured, resumed, and monitored by the platform.

## Non-Negotiables
- Do not show mastery level labels or numeric grades to learners.
- Do not include YouTube players.
- Do not put global unit progress inside iframe content; the platform owns global progress.
- Use responsive iframe content: width `100%`, target 16:9, computers and tablets, mouse and touch support.
- Practice components must have no more than 15 exercises. Middle-school videos must be no longer than 2 minutes.
